title: 阳光下的罪恶
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\117-阳光下的罪恶-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
At the Pirate's Flag Hotel, Mrs. Castle expresses concern to Colonel Weston and various inspectors regarding the reputation of her inn following the strangulation of Mrs. Marshall. She explains her methods for keeping unauthorized people off the island, noting that the property is cut off by the tide and protected by a gated jetty and impassable rocks. However, Colgate suggests that intruders could use small boats to land at a cove. Mrs. Castle identifies her staff, including George, who manages the beach and guests, and William, the gardener. She also lists the hotel employees: four maids, a barman named Henry, a cook with two assistants, and a chief waiter named Albert with three assistants.

Colonel Weston then interviews Kenneth Marshall, the second husband of the deceased Mrs. Marshall (formerly Helen Stewart, a retired actress). Marshall states he and his wife had been married for four years. He claims that while his wife's beauty and acting career likely attracted envy and jealousy from other women, he knows of no men who bore her ill will. He mentions that she had met a Mr. Redfern once at a cocktail party but is unsure about other guests. Marshall reports that the last time he saw his wife was around nine o'clock that morning; she was in her room opening mail, which she noted consisted of bills. He adds that she typically eats breakfast in bed and usually does not come downstairs until near eleven o'clock. Hercule Poirot observes that if she had appeared at ten o'clock, it would have been unusual.
